name: Expand Around Center
summary: Treat every index (and every gap) as a palindrome center and grow two pointers outward while the characters match.
visualizationIntent: init: draw the string as a row of character cells with start and end both at index 0. loop: a purple center pointer walks left to right; for each center the left (blue) and right (amber) pointers begin together. move: left and right glide outward one cell at a time while the characters match, tinting the growing palindrome interior gold and flashing the compared pair. check: compare the length of the palindrome just grown to the best found so far. update: when it is longer, the best span from start to end turns green. return: the longest palindromic substring appears in the result panel.

lineExplanations.3: Measure the length of the string so the pointers know their bounds.
lineExplanations.4: start marks the beginning index of the best palindrome found so far.
lineExplanations.5: end marks the last index of the best palindrome found so far.
lineExplanations.6: Try every index in the string as a possible palindrome center.
lineExplanations.7: Run the expansion twice per center: offset 0 for an odd-length center, offset 1 for an even-length gap.
lineExplanations.8: Place the left pointer on the center character.
lineExplanations.9: Place the right pointer on the center (odd) or the very next character (even).
lineExplanations.10: Keep expanding while both pointers stay in bounds and the characters they point at are equal.
lineExplanations.11: The characters matched, so move the left pointer one step further out.
lineExplanations.12: The characters matched, so move the right pointer one step further out.
lineExplanations.13: Check whether the palindrome we just grew is longer than the best one so far.
lineExplanations.14: It is longer, so record its start index, just inside where the left pointer stopped.
lineExplanations.15: Record its end index, just inside where the right pointer stopped.
lineExplanations.16: Slice out the best palindrome using its recorded start and end indices.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: defines the class wrapper LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def longestPalindrome(self, s): defines the method; s is the input string.
syntaxExplanations.3: len(s) returns the number of characters in s.
syntaxExplanations.4: start = 0 initializes an integer variable to 0.
syntaxExplanations.5: end = 0 initializes another integer variable to 0.
syntaxExplanations.6: for center in range(n): loops center over 0, 1, ..., n-1.
syntaxExplanations.7: for offset in range(2): loops offset over 0 then 1.
syntaxExplanations.8: left = center copies the center index into the variable left.
syntaxExplanations.9: right = center + offset sets right to the center or the next index.
syntaxExplanations.10: while A and B and C: repeats while every condition is true; 'and' stops early if one is false.
syntaxExplanations.13: if condition: runs the indented block only when the condition is true.
syntaxExplanations.14: start = left + 1 assigns a new value to start.
syntaxExplanations.15: end = right - 1 assigns a new value to end.
syntaxExplanations.16: s[start:end + 1] slices out the substring from start to end inclusive.
syntaxExplanations.17: return result hands the answer back to the caller.
